readings.wow: w/w sharply higher — Hormuz re-escalation reverses July trough
readings.source: CBOT Urea Granular FOB Egypt Aug-26 futures indicated ~$475/t as of 21–22 Jul per CME Group / TradingView / Investing.com search snippets (direct page reads returned 403; physical spot paywalled at Profercy/QCIntel/ICIS). Physical spot likely brackets somewhat below the futures level. Consistent with the renewed Hormuz supply-risk premium — US strikes on Iran entering day 10, Qatari LNG disruption, Hormuz tanker traffic at a standstill — reversing the post-7-Jul Egypt ad valorem duty supply release. Physical spot unconfirmed; subscription-grade assessments required for confirmed level.

readings.wow: holding — July contract confirmed; August not yet settled
readings.source: July Yara–Mosaic Tampa CFR contract confirmed at ~$800/t (up $25/t from June's $775/t) — held from prior week. No confirmed August settlement as of 22 Jul; QCIntel and Profercy headlines suggest a subsequent price move after a delayed settlement but no public figure confirmed. Trammo NW Europe CFR traded ~$680/mt in this period — not the Tampa benchmark. Monthly benchmark — paywalled for confirmed assessment.

CBOT DAP FOB NOLA Aug-26 futures indicated ~$757.50/st as of ~22 Jul per Investing.com search snippet (direct page returned 403) — consistent with prior week's indication. Physical barge spot (Green Markets / DTN) remains paywalled. China phosphate export restrictions through August continue to support prices. Indicative — not a confirmed physical assessment.

No fresh public citations for week of 22 Jul — holding prior indications (~$405–415/t CFR). World Bank Pink Sheet March 2026 data: $380.6/t; general market trend ~$400/t per public price-watch sources; Brazil CFR typically carries a premium. Not exchange-traded; subscription-only for confirmed level.

readings.wow: w/w sharply higher — extending Hormuz-driven rally to 4-month highs
readings.source: Front-month closed ~€59.39/MWh on 21 Jul (per tradingeconomics.com search snippets; direct page returned 403), with intraday highs of ~€60.5 — a 4-month high. Up ~13% from the €51–53 range of 15 Jul. Driven by a tenth consecutive day of US airstrikes on Iran, retaliatory Iranian attacks on neighbouring states, and Strait of Hormuz tanker traffic at a near-standstill, sustaining an acute LNG supply-risk premium. EU storage refill continues to lag year-ago levels, amplifying the premium.

editorialReading: TTF has entered a new phase — and the implications for EU nitrogen production are acute. Front-month gas closed near €59.40/MWh on 21 July, a four-month high, as a tenth day of US airstrikes on Iran combined with retaliatory Iranian attacks on neighbouring states and a near-complete halt in Strait of Hormuz tanker traffic. The move up from €51–53/MWh last week represents a further ~13% surge on top of what was already a three-month high. Above €57/MWh, BASF, Yara and OCI face acute plant-idling pressure: European natural gas represents ~70–80% of ammonia production costs, and at these levels importing finished product is consistently cheaper than running European assets. EU storage continues to lag year-ago levels, offering no buffer against seasonal demand or a further escalation. Urea has also reversed course: CBOT FOB Egypt Aug-26 futures indicate a recovery to ~$450–475/t from the ~$385–400 July trough, as renewed Hormuz supply-risk premiums more than offset Egypt's ad valorem duty release. The July Tampa ammonia contract holds at ~$800/t. These two signals — dearer gas and re-firming urea — are now working in the same direction, squeezing EU nitrogen economics from both sides simultaneously.
watchNext: Whether TTF sustains above €57/MWh into the autumn 2026 application window — if so, EU plant-idling pressure returns and import dependence deepens. Whether August Tampa ammonia settles above July's ~$800/t on continued North African tightening and the renewed Hormuz premium. Whether urea consolidates in the ~$450–475/t range or extends the bounce. India / China urea tender activity and any export curbs remain the physical swing factor. Sourcing note: real-time Argus / ICIS / Green Markets assessments are subscription-only and the free World Bank Pink Sheet lags ~a month — these are publicly-cited indications.
methodologyNote: Weekly editorial refresh from World Bank Pink Sheet (monthly, free), Argus public citations, Reuters / Bloomberg quotes, IFA quarterly summaries, and USDA fertilizer outlook. Gold-standard real-time prices (CRU, Argus, ICIS, Profercy) are paywalled — ranges are aggregated from publicly-cited figures and CBOT futures snippets, not republished feeds. Editorial reading is our market interpretation.
